prompt:
Ultra-realistic portrait of a beautiful Korean woman standing near the back of the classroom, a vintage 35mm film camera raised to her eye, looking through the viewfinder — photographing something outside the frame. One eye closed, one focused into the lens. Expression concentrated, quiet, self-contained. Camera (our camera) positioned to her side and slightly behind at a three-quarter angle, capturing her in profile.
Wearing a camel-colored vintage corduroy blazer over a soft white crew-neck tee, tucked into high-waisted dark brown straight-leg trousers. Dark brown leather belt, matching brown leather loafers. Thin wire-frame round glasses. Long dark brown hair pulled into a relaxed low bun with a tortoiseshell clip, a few strands loose at the temples. A well-worn canvas camera bag on the desk behind her.
The golden afternoon light is the subject and the setup simultaneously — she is photographing it. The light streams in from the classroom windows directly into her lens, creating a warm backlit flare around the camera body. Her camel blazer glows like aged amber in the golden hour. Dust motes float beautifully in the shaft of light she is aiming at.
35mm analog film grain, Kodak Portra 400 — camel corduroy and dark brown trousers rendered in deep, lush Portra earth tones. Hyper-realistic corduroy fabric texture, vintage camera metal and glass detail, lens flare. Shallow depth of field. Mood: the photographer inside the photograph, self-referential and quietly poetic, editorial with intellectual texture.
